task_completion.description: Create a nominal variable named 'Passed' based on the 'Exam' score.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Jamovi data spreadsheet shows a new column named 'Passed' containing values 1 (where Exam >= 50) and 0 (where Exam < 50).

task_completion.description: Set up a hierarchical binomial logistic regression with the correct blocks.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Jamovi analysis setup panel shows 'Binomial Logistic Regression' with 'Passed' as the Dependent Variable, 'Revise' in Block 1 of the Model Builder, and 'Anxiety' added in Block 2.

task_completion.description: Generate the logistic regression results including AIC and Odds Ratios.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Jamovi results panel displays the output for the Binomial Logistic Regression, specifically showing Model Fit Measures (containing AIC) and Model Coefficients (containing Odds Ratios).

task_completion.description: Save the Jamovi project to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Jamovi window title bar displays 'ExamPass_LogReg.omv' or a file manager shows the file saved in the ~/Documents/Jamovi/ directory.

task_completion.description: Record the final model's AIC and the Odds Ratio for 'Anxiety' in model_results.txt.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor shows 'model_results.txt' open with the AIC value (approximately 115.8) on the first line and the Odds Ratio for Anxiety (approximately 0.9485) on the second line.

integrity.description: The agent used the Jamovi graphical user interface to perform the analysis.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Jamovi application window, menus, and panels, rather than executing R/Python scripts in a terminal to bypass the GUI.

integrity.description: The recorded results match the actual output generated in Jamovi.
integrity.visual_evidence: The values typed into model_results.txt visually match the AIC and Odds Ratio values displayed in the Jamovi results panel.

integrity.description: The agent actually created and edited the text file.
integrity.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., gedit, nano, mousepad) is visible on screen editing the model_results.txt file, rather than the agent just outputting the text to the terminal.
